title: Project Atlas - Architecture Design Document
author: John Smith
content:
Project Atlas Architecture Design Document

Overview:
Project Atlas is a cloud migration initiative to move the organization's core platform from on-premise infrastructure to AWS cloud. The architecture follows a microservices pattern with centralized API management.

Architecture Components:
1. API Gateway (Kong) - Central entry point for all API traffic
2. Authentication Service - OAuth 2.0 with JWT tokens
3. User Service - User management and profiles
4. Analytics Service - Real-time metrics and reporting
5. Notification Service - Email and push notifications
6. Data Pipeline - ETL processes for analytics

Infrastructure:
- AWS EKS for container orchestration
- RDS PostgreSQL for relational data
- ElastiCache Redis for caching and sessions
- S3 for object storage
- CloudWatch for monitoring

Deployment Strategy:
- Blue-green deployment for zero-downtime releases
- GitOps workflow with ArgoCD
- Automated rollback on failed health checks

Security:
- OAuth 2.0 with PKCE for web apps
- mTLS for service-to-service communication
- Secrets management with AWS Secrets Manager
- Network policies for pod-to-pod communication

title: Deployment Guide - Production Environment
author: Lisa Wang
content:
Production Deployment Guide

Pre-deployment Checklist:
1. All integration tests passing in staging
2. Load test results reviewed and approved
3. Database migration scripts tested
4. Rollback procedure verified
5. On-call team notified

Deployment Steps:
1. Create new deployment manifest with updated image tags
2. Apply database migrations (Alembic upgrade)
3. Deploy to green environment
4. Run smoke tests against green environment
5. Switch traffic from blue to green (Kong upstream update)
6. Monitor for 30 minutes
7. If stable, decommission blue environment

Rollback Procedure:
1. Switch traffic back to blue environment (< 1 minute)
2. Revert database migrations if needed (Alembic downgrade)
3. Notify team via #incidents channel
4. Create incident report

Monitoring:
- Watch CloudWatch dashboards for latency spikes
- Check error rates in application logs
- Verify all health check endpoints returning 200
- Monitor database connection pool usage

title: Production Runbook
author: Lisa Wang
content:
Production Runbook

Common Scenarios:

1. High API Latency
- Check database connection pool (threshold: 80%)
- Check Redis cache hit ratio (expected: >90%)
- Review slow query log
- Scale up pods if CPU > 70%

2. Service Unavailable (5xx errors)
- Check pod status: kubectl get pods -n production
- Review recent deployments: kubectl rollout history
- Check upstream health in Kong admin API
- Initiate rollback if recent deployment caused issue

3. Database Connection Issues
- Check RDS instance status in AWS console
- Verify connection pool settings
- Check network security groups
- Failover to read replica if primary is down

4. Authentication Failures
- Verify OAuth service is running
- Check JWT signing key rotation
- Review token expiration settings
- Check Redis session store connectivity

Escalation Path:
1. On-call engineer (PagerDuty)
2. Team lead (Lisa Wang)
3. Engineering Director
4. VP Engineering (P1 incidents only)

title: API Specification - Project Atlas v3.2
author: Mike Johnson
content:
Project Atlas API Specification v3.2

Base URL: https://api.projectatlas.com/v3

Authentication:
- Bearer token (JWT) in Authorization header
- OAuth 2.0 token endpoint: POST /auth/token
- Token refresh: POST /auth/refresh

Endpoints:

Users:
- GET /users - List users (admin scope)
- GET /users/{id} - Get user profile
- PUT /users/{id} - Update user profile

Projects:
- GET /projects - List projects
- POST /projects - Create project (write scope)
- GET /projects/{id} - Get project details
- PUT /projects/{id} - Update project

Analytics:
- GET /analytics/dashboard - Dashboard metrics
- GET /analytics/reports - Generate reports
- POST /analytics/custom - Custom query

Rate Limits:
- 1000 requests/minute per authenticated user
- 100 requests/minute for unauthenticated requests
- Rate limit headers included in all responses

title: Requirements Document - Release 3.2
author: Sarah Chen
content:
Release 3.2 Requirements Document

Functional Requirements:

FR-001: API Gateway
- Must route requests to appropriate microservices
- Must support rate limiting (1000 req/min/user)
- Must terminate SSL at gateway level
- Must support health check endpoints for all services

FR-002: OAuth 2.0 Authentication
- Must support Authorization Code with PKCE flow
- Must support Client Credentials flow
- Must issue JWT tokens with RSA-256 signing
- Must support token refresh with sliding expiration
- Must maintain backward compatibility with API key auth

FR-003: New Dashboard
- Must display real-time metrics
- Must support customizable widgets
- Must work on mobile devices
- Must load within 2 seconds

Non-Functional Requirements:

NFR-001: Performance
- P95 latency < 500ms for all API endpoints
- System must handle 10,000 concurrent users

NFR-002: Availability
- 99.9% uptime SLA
- Zero-downtime deployments
- 15-minute maximum rollback time

NFR-003: Security
- All data encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3)
- All secrets stored in AWS Secrets Manager
- Audit logging for all admin operations
